title: Environment, Shell State, Startup Files, and Sessions · Transfer Runbook
summary: Repair a command that works interactively but fails in automation by tracing environment and startup differences instead of adding global hacks. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of an environment-diff and startup-resolution notebook with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish shell variables, exported environment entries, positional parameters, and child-process inheritance.
Trace aliases, functions, builtins, hashed commands, PATH search, and explicit paths with type and command evidence.
Identify login, interactive, non-interactive, and remote shell startup paths before editing configuration.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.
